chandler-venice-906.extract: Venice is a city in northeastern Italy and the capital of the region of Veneto. It is built on a group of 126 islands that are separated by expanses of open water and by canals; portions of the city are linked by 472 bridges.
chandler-venice-906.romanEraExtract: The Venetian lagoon was inhabited since Roman times, with scattered settlements on the islands serving as refuges during barbarian invasions of the 5th century. The area was part of the Roman province of Venetia et Histria, and the mainland cities of Aquileia and Altinum were the major Roman centers in the region. As mainland Roman cities fell to the Lombards in the 6th century, refugees increasingly settled on the lagoon islands, laying the foundations for what would become Venice.

21803.extract: Ani is a ruined medieval Armenian city now situated in Turkey's province of Kars, next to the closed border with Armenia.
21803.romanEraExtract: Ani is a ruined medieval Armenian city now situated in Turkey's province of Kars, next to the closed border with Armenia.

32702.extract: Tbilisi is the capital and largest city of Georgia, located on the banks of the Kura River. With more than 1.3 million inhabitants, it contains almost one third of the country's population. Tbilisi was founded in the 5th century CE by Vakhtang I of Iberia and has since served as the capital of various Georgian kingdoms and republics. Between 1801 and 1917, then part of the Russian Empire, it was the seat of the Caucasus Viceroyalty, governing both the northern and southern sides of the Caucasus.
32702.romanEraExtract: Tbilisi is the capital and largest city of Georgia, located on the banks of the Kura River. With more than 1.3 million inhabitants, it contains almost one third of the country's population. Tbilisi was founded in the 5th century CE by Vakhtang I of Iberia and has since served as the capital of various Georgian kingdoms and republics. 23085.extract: Pons Aelius, or Newcastle Roman Fort, was an auxiliary castra and small Roman settlement on Hadrian's Wall in the Roman province of Britannia Inferior, situated on the north bank of the River Tyne close to the centre of present-day Newcastle upon Tyne, and occupied between the 2nd and 4th centuries AD.
23085.romanEraExtract: Pons Aelius was a Roman fort and settlement on Hadrian's Wall in the province of Britannia Inferior, situated on the north bank of the River Tyne at the site of modern Newcastle upon Tyne. Built under Emperor Hadrian around 122 AD as part of the frontier wall system, the fort guarded a bridge crossing the Tyne. The name Pons Aelius ('Bridge of Aelius') derives from Hadrian's family name. The fort housed an auxiliary garrison and was part of the chain of fortifications stretching from the Solway Firth to Wallsend.

33884.extract: Baghdad is the capital and largest city in Iraq. It is located on the banks of the Tigris in central Iraq. The city has an estimated population of 8 million. It ranks among the most populous and largest cities in the Middle East and the Arab world and constitutes 22% of Iraq's population. Baghdad is a primary financial and commercial center in the region.
33884.romanEraExtract: Baghdad is the capital and largest city in Iraq. It is located on the banks of the Tigris in central Iraq. The city has an estimated population of 8 million. It ranks among the most populous and largest cities in the Middle East and the Arab world and constitutes 22% of Iraq's population. Baghdad is a primary financial and commercial center in the region.

chandler-merv-1100.extract: Merv, also called the Merve Oasis, was a major Iranian city in Central Asia, located on the historical Silk Road, near today's Mary, Turkmenistan. Human settlements on the site of Merv existed from the 3rd millennium BC until the 18th century AD. It changed hands repeatedly throughout history. Under the Achaemenid Empire, it was the center of the satrapy of Margiana. It was subsequently ruled by Hellenistic Kings, Parthians, Sasanians, Arabs, Ghaznavids, Seljuqs, Khwarazmians and Timurids, among others.
chandler-merv-1100.romanEraExtract: Merv (ancient Antiochia Margiana or Alexandria Margiana) was a major oasis city on the Silk Road in Central Asia, in modern Turkmenistan. Originally founded as a Persian satrapal capital, it was refounded by Alexander the Great and later expanded by the Seleucid king Antiochus I. Under the Parthian Empire it became one of the great cities of the east, and it remained significant under the Sasanians. At its peak it was one of the largest cities in the world, serving as a vital node in trans-Asian trade connecting China, India, Persia, and the Roman Empire.

38.extract: Traiectum was a Roman fort, on the frontier of the Roman Empire in Germania Inferior. The remains of the fort are in the center of Utrecht, Netherlands, which takes its name from the fort.
